texts.full_text: key focus of her research is learning how social networks might shape or be shaped by how our brains process information. Her previous work explored how the brain encodes one’s social standing, or where one sits in relation to another within a social hierarchy. She now wanted to understand how friendship itself was fleshed out in the brain. Parkinson and her co-authors, Adam Kleinbaum and Thalia Wheatley of Dartmouth College, set out to investigate whether the similarity in neural activity during the processing of dynamic social situations could predict friendships. To test this hypothesis, they recruited 42 graduate students from a business school and had them watch a series of video clips while undergoing functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI). The videos covered a wide range of topics and genres, including comedy, documentaries, and science videos, to capture various emotional and cognitive responses. The researchers then compared the brain activity patterns of these individuals to see if friends had more similar neural responses. Their findings were striking: friends showed significantly more similarity in their neural activity patterns when watching the videos compared to non-friends. This similarity was particularly pronounced in brain regions associated with mentalizing (thinking about others' thoughts and feelings), attention, and emotional responding. In essence, the study revealed that friends have a more synchronized way of processing information, suggesting a deeper cognitive and emotional alignment. The implications of this research are profound. It suggests that friendship is not just a matter of shared interests or convenience but is rooted in how our brains process and respond to the world around us. This neural similarity might explain why we feel comfortable and understood with our friends, as they literally see the world more like we do. Parkinson’s work opens up new avenues for understanding social bonds and could have applications in various fields, from social psychology to neuroscience, and even in improving social interactions in diverse environments such as workplaces and schools.

texts.full_text: in Republika Srpska, an entity of Bosnia and Herzegovina. It is situated in the southern part of the Posavina region. Asof 2013, it has a population of 4,667 inhabitants. The small municipality was created from part of the pre-warVukosavlje Municipality encompasses an area rich with history and natural beauty; known for its fertile lands that support agriculture as well local businesses including traditional crafts like pottery making which has been passed down through generations within families living in this region. The village also features several preserved historical sites such us Orthodox churches dating back centuries showcasing architectural styles representative of the Serbian tradition during those times while serving their primary purpose today - providing spiritual guidance and community gatherings for residents alike throughout different seasons & events marking significant milestones on annual calendars . Beyond religious significance, there are many outdoor activities one can partake in whilst visiting Vukosavlje. One notable attraction is its vast forest areas perfect both leisure walks or adventurous hikes with stunning panoramic views overlooking the landscape dotted by small rural settlements peppered across fields stretching towards distant hills beyond which lies Serbia’s borderline further northwards . Additionally, fishing enthusiasts can indulge their hobby here as well since there are several rivers and lakes nearby where one could find peace whilst casting lines into crystal clear waters in hopes of catching dinner for the day. All these experiences combined make Vukosavlje not just another stopover on tourist itineraries but a destination unto itself worth visiting to appreciate what this small municipality has preserved over time amidst changing political landscapes and modernization pressures experienced globally

texts.full_text: New" blog for the Critiques Of Libertarianism website. Saturday, September 06, 2014 Recent Quotations Here are a bunch of the most recently added quotations. The red lines are the categories I've filed these in. More deeply though there's something about how the rhetoric of 'freedom' and 'liberty' appeals to the 'neo-Confederate' mindset which is paradoxical and considerably more toxic and corrosive than the ways many of us think about those terms. Freedom can also mean freedom from any check on my actions. My freedom. My group's freedom. A warlord who totally dominates his followers has a sort of perfect liberty and freedom. Just not quite the sort we think of in a civic context. It's the same authoritarian mindset of Stormfront and the militia crazies, just through this looking glass where it twists into 'freedom' and 'liberty'. Josh Marshall, "Keeping It Real on 'neo-Confederate Libertarians'" Freedom (propaganda), Josh Marshall, Keeping It Real on 'neo-Confederate Libertarians', Liberty (propaganda), Quotations, Racists What has been created by this half century of massive corporate propaganda is what's called "anti-politics". So that anything that goes wrong, you blame the government, you blame the politicians, you blame the system, you blame the bureaucrats, you blame the "elites". So, you've created this massive, half century of propaganda that's created this "anti-politics".

It should be noted that, for convenience, we are using the word "solution" to describe the aqueous composition obtained by mixing the polymer with water and allowing the mixture to reach substantial equilibrium even though the "solution" necessarily contains undissolved microbeads. Accordingly we use the word "solution" throughout this specification to indicate a substantially equilibrated aqueous composition irrespective of whether it is a true solution or an aqueous dispersion of undissolved microbeads or larger beads. We are aware of the commercialisation of materials for a microparticulate paper making process in which the final stage involves the addition of cross linked water insoluble microbeads under the trade name Polyflex CS and which we believe may be intended to be within the scope of U.S. Pat. No. 5,167,766 and U.S. Pat. No. 5,274,055. Scanning electron microscope examination of equilibrated solutions of Polyflex CS clearly shows the presence of substantially spherical undissolved particles having a size below 750 nm. This provides further confirmation that these two patents are intended to provide a water insoluble, microparticulate, retention system in somewhat similar manner to those provided previously using insoluble bentonite or silica sol. Thus, the cross linking of the particles prevents the particles dissolving into true solution during equilibration with water and instead they retain a visible microparticulate form, which is in the form of solid particles when dried. texts.full_text: communication network for users. An access network connects subscribers with service providers. A communication network may have multiple access networks, serving different sets of users, in communication with a single core network. Typically, a user device requests a certain piece of content, such as a web page or a file, from the network. The content may be located on an electronic device, such as a file server, which may be located in the local access network, the core network, or a different access network. The request for content is typically forwarded through the access network to the core network. Within the access network, a number of devices may facilitate the forwarding or delivery of requests and information from a user to the core network. A user typically interacts with an access network through a base station that receives information and requests wirelessly via radio waves before transmitting them further within the communication system. This could involve cellular networks, Wi-Fi hotspots, satellite links—or any combination

texts.full_text: put: <Vector (0.176471, 0.294118)> Donde Vector es la clase siguiente: class Vector(object): def __init__(self, x, y): self.x = x self.y = y A: Todo en Python es un objeto. Cada objeto tiene algunos métodos internos especiales que utiliza para interactuar con otros objetos. En general, estos métodos siguen la convención de nomenclatura __action__. En conjunto, esto se denomina modelo de datos de Python. Puede sobrecargar cualquiera de estos métodos. Esto se usa comúnmente en la sobrecarga del operador en Python. A coPara sobrescargar los operadores en Python, puedes definir métodos especiales dentro de tu clase que correspondan a las operaciones deseadas. Aquí tienes un ejemplo completo para la clase `Vector`: ```python class Vector(object): def __init__(self, x, y): self.x = x self.y = y # Sobrecarga del operador de suma (+) def __add__(self, other): if isinstance(other, Vector): return Vector(self.x + other.x, self.y + other.y) raise TypeError("Operación no soportada para tipos de datos incompatibles") # Sobrecarga del operador de resta (-) def __sub__(self, other): if isinstance(other, Vector): return Vector(self.x - other.x, self.y - other.y) raise TypeError("Operación no soportada para tipos de datos incompatibles") # Sobrecarga del operador de multiplicación (*) def __mul__(self, scalar): if isinstance(scalar, (int, float)): # Asegúrate que el escalar sea un número return Vector(self.x scalar) raise TypeError("Operación no soportada para tipos de datos incompatibles") # Sobrecarga del operador de división (/) def __truediv__(self, scalar): if isinstance(scalar, (int, float)): # Asegúrate que el escalar sea un número return Vector(self.x / scalar, self.y / scalar) raise TypeError("Operación no soportada para tipos de datos incompatibles") # Sobrecarga del operador de división entera (//) def __floordiv__(self, scalar): if isinstance(scalar, (int, float)): # Asegúrate que el escalar sea un número return Vector(self.x // scalar, self.y // scalar) raise TypeError("Operación no soportada para tipos de datos incompatibles") def __str__(self): return f"<Vector ({self.x:.6f}, {self.y:.6f})>" # Ejemplo de uso a = Vector(3, 5) b = Vector(2, 7) print(a + b) # Output: <Vector (5.000000, 12.000000)> print(b - a) # Output: <Vector (-1.000000, 2.000000)> print(b * 1.3) # Output: <Vector (2.600000, 9.100000)> print(a // 17) # Output: <Vector (0.000000, 0.000000)> print

texts.full_text: in the development of macro and microvascular complications that are the leading causes of morbidity and mortality in diabetic patients all over the world. Recent studies have shown that crystallins play an instrumental role in diabetes and its complications. Therefore, this review highlights the current data on the impact of chronic hyperglycemia on expression, distribution, glycation, phosphorylation, chaperone-like function and, anti-apoptotic activity of crystallins. Furthermore, we discussed the insights for developing therapeutic strategies for diabetic complications including natural agents, peptides, and pharmacological chaperones that modulate or mimic chaperone activity of α-crystallins. Upregulation of crystallins appears to be a common feature of chronic diabetes. Further, cataractogenesis is associated with increased expression levels of β-crystallin in the lens epithelium as well as its accumulation on the surface of cells and extracellularly within the anterior chamber. texts.full_text: July 2016. The psychometric criteria used to assess these studies were adapted from published recommendations in accordance with US Food and Drug Administration guidance. Nine studies were included which reported on aspects of the development and/or validation of one generic (36-Item Short Form Health Survey, SF-36®) and three disease-specific (Aberdeen Varicose Vein Questionnaire, AVVQ; Varicose Veins Symptoms Questionnaire, VVSymQ®; Specific Quality-of-life and Outcome Response - Venous, SQOR-V) PROMs. The evidence from included studies provided data to support the construct validity, test-retest reliability and responsiveness of the AVVQ. However, its content validity, including weighting of the AVVQ questions, was biased and based on the opinion of clinicians, and the instrument had poor acceptability. VVSymQ® displayed good responsiveness and acceptability rates. SF-36® was considered to have satisfactory responsiveness and internal consistency. texts.full_text: o traditional cryptographic methods. This difference introduces unique challenges and considerations: 1. Infrastructure Requirements: Quantum key distribution systems require specialized hardware for photon generation, transmission over optical fibers or free space links with minimal loss, single-photon detectors capable of high efficiency under low-light conditions, etc. 2. Distance Limitations: Current QKD technologies are limited by the distance photons can travel through a medium (like fiber optics) without losing coherence and thus compromising security due to photon decoherence effects such as absorption or scattering. 3. Implementation Complexity: Implementing quantum cryptographic systems involves complex integration of hardware with classical computing resources, necessitating sophisticated protocols for error correction and privacy amplification in order to ensure secure key generation even when some information about the keys is leaked through side channels during transmission over noisy environments like optical fibers where losses occur naturally or are induced by potential eavesdroppers. 4. Scalability Issues: Scaling up QKD networks involves not only expanding physical infrastructure but also developing robust network architectures that can efficiently distribute quantum-secured communication services across a wide geographic area, possibly incorporating satellite-based solutions to overcome terrestrial distance limitations and achieve global connectivity securely using photons traveling through space vacuum with minimal attenuation. 5. Standardization Efforts: There is an ongoing need for standardizing QKD protocols at various levels including physical layer specifications of hardware components up till application-level security assurance mechanisms so that different systems from multiple vendors can interoperate seamlessly ensuring interoperability and long-term sustainability in deployment across diverse network topologies ranging from point-to-point links to extensive metropolitan or global networks. 6. Economic Feasibility: The high costs associated with the development, maintenance of quantum cryptographic technologies pose significant economic barriers hindering widespread adoption unless cost-effective solutions can be developed through advances in manufacturing processes and improvements yielding economies-of-scale benefits over time alongside advancements reducing component prices making it more viable for broader commercial use

texts.full_text: “I love the (daddy) club,” Kiedis said.“Good God, it’s a beautiful club. Whoknew? You can’t know until you go. It’s wild,” said Kiedis, who is busy promoting "I'm With You," the band's newest CD released on Tuesday. Kiedis, 48, has a 3½-year-old son, Everly Bear. And he loves spending time with his little man. He said some people think having children means the end of fun or creativity, but for Kiedis, it’s quite the opposite. "It's reignited a sense of wonder andjoy in my life," he added. "Being a dadhas made me more present, more connected to the world around me, and it’s influencedmy music and art in profound ways."Kiedis also mentioned that balancing family life with touring and promoting the new album is challenging but rewarding, emphasizing the importance of quality time with his son whenever possible.

texts.full_text: intensity of the semiconductor laser is set to a desired value. Various kinds of APC (Automatic Power Control) circuits have been proposed to solve this problem and use the advantages of the semiconductor laser. These APC circuits are generally divided into the following three systems. (1) In a first system, the light output of the semiconductor laser is monitored by a light-receiving element. A photoelectric negative feedback loop for controlling a forward electric current of the semiconductor laser at any time is disposed such that a signal proportional to a light-receiving electric current (proportional to the light output of the semiconductor laser) generated in this light-receiving element is equal to a command signal indicative of a luminous level. In this system, the light output of the semiconductor laser is controlled to be a desired value by this photoelectric negative feedback loop. (2) In a second system, the light output of the semiconductor laser is monitored by the light-receiving element in a power setting period. The forward electric current of the semiconductor laser is controlled such that the signal proportional to the light-receiving electric current (proportional to the light output of the semiconductor laser) generated in this light-receiving element is equal to the luminous level command signal. In a period except for the power setting period, the forward electric current value of the semiconductor laser set in the power setting period is held to control the light output of the semiconductor laser to be a desired value.
